title: Walking Through Madrid

story.location: The streets between the café and Plaza Mayor
story.scene: Carlos steps outside the café and points toward the old streets, but he does not lead. He asks you to find Plaza Mayor by speaking with a local and following three short direction cues.
story.mission: Today you’ll ask where Plaza Mayor is, understand three simple directions, and identify the destination.
story.continuity: The walk begins immediately after Lesson 6. Carlos briefly acknowledges the learner’s stored drink preference, then steps back so the learner can navigate.

emotionalArc.startsWith: The learner expects Carlos to lead through the unfamiliar streets.
emotionalArc.endsWith: The learner reaches the plaza by asking, listening, and making the final navigation decision independently.
emotionalArc.earnedBy: Understanding a short real-world route after Carlos deliberately stops giving the answers.

coffee.spanish: Antes de caminar, ¿quieres terminar tu café?
coffee.english: Before we walk, do you want to finish your coffee?

tea.spanish: Antes de caminar, ¿quieres terminar tu té?
tea.english: Before we walk, do you want to finish your tea?

juice.spanish: Antes de caminar, ¿quieres terminar tu zumo?
juice.english: Before we walk, do you want to finish your juice?

microCliffhanger.speaker: Carlos
microCliffhanger.spanish: Empieza a llover. Estamos cerca de mi apartamento. ¿Quieres verlo?
microCliffhanger.english: It’s starting to rain. We’re close to my apartment. Would you like to see it?

carlosIntroduction.title: You lead the way
carlosIntroduction.message: Plaza Mayor is close, but I’m not going to guide us this time. You’ll ask a local where it is, listen for three short street cues, and tell me when we’ve arrived. I’ll help only if communication breaks down.
carlosIntroduction.prompt: Begin with: Perdón, ¿dónde está Plaza Mayor?

canDo: Understand right, left, and straight ahead.
Recognize simple location phrases with está.
Thank someone for directions and identify the destination.
objectives: Ask a local where Plaza Mayor is.
Follow three short direction cues in order.
Use está to say where a singular place is.
Complete the route without Carlos giving the answer.

recycledLanguage.use: Approach a local and close the exchange politely.
Understand the sequence of the route.
Begin outside the place the learner just visited.

essentialPhrases.exampleEnglish: Excuse me, where is Plaza Mayor?

essentialPhrases.usage: Say a place is at the current location.
Point to a place farther away.
Recognize a right turn or right-side location.
Recognize a left turn or left-side location.
Locate a place using a familiar landmark.
essentialPhrases.exampleSpanish: La plaza está al lado de la cafetería.
essentialPhrases.exampleEnglish: The plaza is beside the café.
Perfect. Thank you for the help.

vocabulary.tip: A plaza is a public square and common meeting place.
Street names often appear on tiled signs in Madrid.
En la esquina means on the corner.
A useful landmark when following directions.
Fountains are common visual landmarks in plazas.
Look for the green pharmacy cross in Spain.
vocabulary.exampleSpanish: La farmacia está a la derecha.
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The pharmacy is on the right.

vocabulary.tip: This returns from the market as a street landmark.
vocabulary.exampleSpanish: La panadería está en la esquina.
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The bakery is on the corner.

vocabulary.tip: The route begins beside the café from Lesson 6.
Useful for future travel; recognize it on signs.
The written accent marks the question word.
Use está for one place or thing.
A useful backup if the full question feels difficult.
Aquí refers to the current place.
Allí points to a place farther away.
Está cerca is a complete useful response.
¿Está lejos? asks whether a place is far.
Follow it with a landmark.
vocabulary.exampleSpanish: Está al lado de la farmacia.

vocabulary.tip: Use it when two places face each other.
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: It is across from the fountain.

vocabulary.tip: Treat this as one fixed street cue.
Connect derecha with your right hand.
Connect izquierda with your left hand.
Recognize it as a direction; command grammar comes later.
Listen for the side that follows.
Recognize it before a street or traffic light.
Use it before stopping someone for help.
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Excuse me, where is the plaza?

vocabulary.tip: A warm closing after receiving directions.
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Perfect. Thank you for the help.

grammar.meaning: Ask where one place is and say where it is located.
grammar.explanation: Use ¿Dónde está + place? to ask for one location. Answer with está plus a simple location phrase: está aquí, está cerca, está a la derecha. Today, está is the productive tool. You may hear hay una plaza to say that a plaza exists; Lesson 8 will compare hay and está directly.

notes.title: Locate
notes.text: Está cerca. Está a la derecha.

notes.title: Use a landmark
notes.text: Está al lado de la farmacia.

notes.title: Preview only
notes.text: Hay una plaza cerca means there is a plaza nearby. Recognize it today; do not replace the está pattern yet.

examples.spanish: La fuente está a la izquierda.
examples.english: The fountain is on the left.

examples.spanish: La farmacia está al lado de la panadería.
examples.english: The pharmacy is beside the bakery.

grammar.practicePrompt: Choose one nearby place and ask where it is, then answer with está.

dialogue.title: Finding Plaza Mayor
dialogue.setting: Outside the café, Carlos lets the learner stop a local resident and follow the route.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.spanish: La plaza está cerca, pero hoy tú guías.
lines.english: The plaza is nearby, but today you lead.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.english: Excuse me, where is Plaza Mayor?

lines.speaker: Vecina
lines.spanish: Después, gira a la derecha en la panadería.
lines.english: Then turn right at the bakery.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.spanish: A la derecha en la panadería.

lines.speaker: Vecina
lines.spanish: Cruza la calle. La plaza está a la izquierda.
lines.english: Cross the street. The plaza is on the left.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.english: And now? Where is the plaza?

comprehension.prompt: Where does the learner turn right?
Where is the plaza at the end?

listening.title: Three cues through the city
listening.instructions: Listen, follow along, then check what you understood.
listening.naturalScript: Está cerca. Sigue todo recto. Después, gira a la derecha en la panadería. Cruza la calle. La plaza está a la izquierda.
listening.slowScript: Está cerca. / Sigue todo recto. / Después, / gira a la derecha / en la panadería. / Cruza la calle. / La plaza está / a la izquierda.

transcript.speaker: Learner
transcript.english: Excuse me, where is Plaza Mayor?

transcript.speaker: Vecina
transcript.english: It is nearby. Continue straight ahead.

transcript.speaker: Vecina
transcript.spanish: Después, gira a la derecha en la panadería.
transcript.english: Then turn right at the bakery.

transcript.speaker: Vecina
transcript.spanish: Cruza la calle. La plaza está a la izquierda.
transcript.english: Cross the street. The plaza is on the left.

transcript.speaker: Carlos
transcript.english: And now? Where is the plaza?

transcript.speaker: Learner
transcript.english: It is there, on the left.

comprehension.prompt: What should the learner do first?
comprehension.explanation: Sigue todo recto is the first route cue.

comprehension.prompt: Where should the learner turn right?
comprehension.explanation: The neighbor says gira a la derecha en la panadería.

comprehension.prompt: What happens immediately before reaching the plaza?
comprehension.explanation: Cruza la calle comes just before the final location cue.

comprehension.prompt: Where is the plaza at the end?
comprehension.explanation: La plaza está a la izquierda means the plaza is on the left.

items.spanish: Gira a la derecha en la panadería.
La plaza está a la izquierda.
options: The plaza is on the left.
The plaza is on the right.
items.answer: The plaza is on the left.

items.spanish: Está al lado de la fuente.

pronunciation.focus: Direction words that must sound different
tips: Keep the middle sound clear: iz-KYER-da.
Keep derecha clear and even: de-RE-cha.
The written accent in dónde marks the question sound.
Pause after Perdón before asking the question.

items.note: Keep both o vowels short and clear.
Stress quier and keep the opening iz short. In much of Spain, z sounds like th in think; in most of Latin America, it sounds like s.
Finish with a warm, relaxed rhythm.

speaking.instructions: Carlos steps back while you ask for and follow the route one response at a time.

items.label: Listen
items.carlosPromptEnglish: Excuse me, where is Plaza Mayor?
items.cue: Hear the complete question once, then ask it yourself.
items.meaning: Excuse me, where is Plaza Mayor?

items.label: Your turn
items.carlosPrompt: Sigue todo recto. Después, gira a la derecha.
items.cue: Repeat the route in two clear chunks.
items.prompt: Repeat the first two route cues.
items.text: Todo recto. Después, a la derecha.
items.meaning: Straight ahead. Then, to the right.

items.label: Keep talking
items.carlosPromptEnglish: The plaza is there. Where is it?
items.cue: Use está and the side the neighbor gave you.
items.text: La plaza está a la izquierda.
items.meaning: The plaza is on the left.
items.focus: Use está for the singular place.

items.label: One more challenge
items.carlosPrompt: Después de la panadería, ¿dónde está la plaza?
items.carlosPromptEnglish: After the bakery, where is the plaza?
items.cue: Use the final direction the neighbor gave you.
items.focus: Keep a la izquierda as one direction chunk.

items.label: Finish the conversation
items.carlosPrompt: ¿Dónde está un lugar cerca de ti?
items.carlosPromptEnglish: Where is a place near you?
items.cue: Ask about one real place, then locate it with aquí, allí, cerca, or a side.
items.prompt: Locate a real place near you.
items.text: ¿Dónde está la farmacia? Está allí, a la derecha.
items.meaning: Where is the pharmacy? It is there, on the right.
items.focus: Replace farmacia with a place true for you.

flashcards.selectionRule: Only Tier 1 landmarks, location chunks, and route cues required by the mission appear.

quiz.explanation: Use ¿Dónde está...? to ask for one place or thing.
Todo recto is the fixed direction cue for straight ahead.

quiz.prompt: Which direction is a la derecha?

quiz.explanation: La esquina means the corner of a street.

quiz.prompt: Which landmark has a green cross in Spain?
quiz.explanation: Spanish pharmacies are commonly marked with a green cross.
Use está to ask where one place is located.

quiz.prompt: Complete: La fuente está a la _____.
quiz.explanation: A la izquierda means on or to the left.

quiz.prompt: The local says Sigue todo recto. What should you do?
quiz.explanation: Sigue todo recto tells you to continue straight ahead.

quiz.prompt: Which phrase locates the pharmacy beside the bakery?
options: La farmacia está al lado de la panadería.
La farmacia está a las diez.
quiz.answer: La farmacia está al lado de la panadería.
quiz.explanation: Está plus al lado de correctly locates the pharmacy beside a landmark.

quiz.prompt: You stop a stranger for help. Which opener is most natural?
quiz.explanation: Perdón politely gets attention before the location question.

quiz.prompt: Translate: Está allí, a la derecha.
options: It is there, on the right.
It is here, on the left.
quiz.answer: It is there, on the right.
quiz.explanation: Allí means there and a la derecha means on the right.

quiz.prompt: The local has finished explaining. What should you say?
quiz.explanation: This thanks the person specifically for helping with the route.

quiz.prompt: Follow the route: todo recto → derecha at the bakery → cross the street. What comes second?
quiz.explanation: The bakery turn is the second cue, after going straight.

options: Perdón, ¿dónde está Plaza Mayor? — Todo recto y a la derecha. — Gracias por la ayuda.
Estoy Plaza Mayor a las diez.
quiz.answer: Perdón, ¿dónde está Plaza Mayor? — Todo recto y a la derecha. — Gracias por la ayuda.
quiz.explanation: It includes a polite opener, a clear location question, useful directions, and thanks.

options: La farmacia está enfrente de la fuente.
quiz.answer: La farmacia está enfrente de la fuente.
quiz.explanation: Está plus enfrente de locates the singular pharmacy across from the fountain.

quiz.prompt: You hear Hay una plaza cerca. What should you understand today?
options: The plaza is on the right.
quiz.explanation: Hay previews existence. Today you only need to recognize it; Lesson 8 will compare hay and está.

quiz.prompt: Carlos asks ¿Dónde está la plaza? You can see it across from the fountain. Answer naturally.
quiz.explanation: Está plus enfrente de precisely identifies the plaza’s location.

quiz.prompt: Carlos stops helping. Which response completes the mission independently?
options: Perdón, ¿dónde está Plaza Mayor? Todo recto, después a la derecha en la panadería, y luego cruzo la calle. Perfecto. Gracias por la ayuda.
Quisiera una plaza y una tostada.
Mi familia está libre el sábado.
quiz.answer: Perdón, ¿dónde está Plaza Mayor? Todo recto, después a la derecha en la panadería, y luego cruzo la calle. Perfecto. Gracias por la ayuda.
quiz.explanation: The learner asks, confirms all three route cues, and closes politely.

miniConversation.title: Find the plaza without Carlos
miniConversation.setting: Carlos stays silent while the learner asks a local resident for the complete route.
miniConversation.goal: Ask for Plaza Mayor, confirm three direction cues, identify it, and thank the local.

turns.speaker: Learner
turns.english: Excuse me, where is Plaza Mayor?

turns.speaker: Vecina
turns.spanish: Gira a la derecha en la panadería.

turns.speaker: Learner
turns.spanish: A la derecha en la panadería.

turns.speaker: Vecina
turns.spanish: Cruza la calle. Está a la izquierda.
turns.english: Cross the street. It is on the left.

turns.speaker: Learner
turns.spanish: Está allí. Gracias por la ayuda.
turns.english: It is there. Thank you for the help.

turns.speaker: Carlos
turns.spanish: ¡Muy bien! Esta vez yo no te he dado las direcciones.
turns.english: Very good! This time I didn’t give you the directions.

culture.title: Finding your way in central Madrid
culture.text: Madrid’s historic center has short streets, irregular corners, and many recognizable landmarks. People often give directions using a bakery, pharmacy, plaza, or traffic light rather than a street number. Begin with perdón, listen for the landmark and direction, repeat the key cue to confirm it, and finish with gracias.

worldBuilding.carlosSpanish: Estas calles son antiguas. A veces un edificio es mejor referencia que el nombre de la calle.
worldBuilding.carlosEnglish: These streets are old. Sometimes a building is a better reference than the street name.

worldBuilding.carlosSpanish: Ese músico toca aquí muchos sábados. La gente del barrio ya lo conoce.
worldBuilding.carlosEnglish: That musician plays here many Saturdays. People in the neighborhood already know him.

worldBuilding.carlosSpanish: Plaza Mayor siempre está llena de historias y de gente.
worldBuilding.carlosEnglish: Plaza Mayor is always full of stories and people.

livingWorldInteractions.title: The tiled street sign
livingWorldInteractions.prompt: A blue-and-white ceramic sign is set into the corner wall. Look closer before turning.
livingWorldInteractions.carlosSpanish: Muchas calles antiguas de Madrid tienen placas de cerámica con dibujos de su historia.
livingWorldInteractions.carlosEnglish: Many old Madrid streets have ceramic signs with pictures from their history.

nativeSpeech.note: A local may point while saying it; the gesture carries part of the meaning.
A common way to describe a short walking distance without giving exact meters.
Useful to recognize, but not required for the mission.

commonMistakes.why: Use está for the location of a place.
Keep a la derecha as one complete direction chunk.

commonMistakes.better: Está al lado de la farmacia.
commonMistakes.why: Al lado de needs de before the landmark.

commonMistakes.better: Connect each word to the matching hand while practicing.
commonMistakes.why: A physical cue makes these similar-looking direction words easier to retrieve.

realLifeMission.title: Carlos Challenge: Find Plaza Mayor
realLifeMission.mission: Carlos stops giving directions. Ask a local where Plaza Mayor is, understand and confirm three route cues, identify the destination, and thank the person who helped.
successCriteria: Use one landmark to confirm the route.
realLifeMission.completionResponse: ¡Lo encontraste! I stayed quiet, and you used Spanish to move through Madrid independently.
realLifeMission.correctionRule: Celebrate reaching the plaza first. Correct only one direction or location error that would have changed the route.
realLifeMission.handoff: Empieza a llover. Estamos cerca de mi apartamento. ¿Quieres verlo?

canDo: I can ask where one place is.
I can understand right, left, and straight ahead.
I can locate a place with está.
I can follow a short route without Carlos translating it.
selfCheck: Can I ask the location question without reading it?
Can I physically respond to each direction cue?
Can I repeat a landmark to confirm what I heard?
Can I identify the destination in Spanish?

passportStamp.title: Madrid Navigator
passportStamp.description: You asked for directions and found Plaza Mayor without Carlos leading the way.
passportStamp.unlockCondition: Complete the Carlos Challenge for Lesson 7.

achievement.title: Found the Way
achievement.description: Follow three Spanish direction cues and identify the destination independently.

carlosClosing: I almost answered for you—but I’m glad I didn’t. You asked for the route, followed every cue, and found Plaza Mayor yourself. Now the rain is starting, and my apartment is close.
